stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 3 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 8 observations with mean 80.8 points (within-school SD = 7.8). school B has 15 observations with mean 58.6 points (within-school SD = 8.1). school C has 5 observations with mean 56.0 points (within-school SD = 9.7). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.8 and 17.0 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school B: 58.8 points (shrunk from raw 58.6). Range [58.7, 59.1] as between-school SD varies from 6.8 to 17.0. Pooled mean: 64.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 64.5 points (weighted average of 3 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=11.3: B = 0.967.
Interval [58.7, 59.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (58.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (64.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
